summary: Matched HSD_ByteCodeEval in src/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c with small signature/header cleanup and an extern update in robj.c. The match involved type normalization to platform typedefs, local-variable ordering/type adjustments, simplifying control-flow artifacts, and reshaping a local fmodf helper to match expected codegen.
what_changed: Changed HSD_ByteCodeEval parameter types from float*/int to f32*/s32 in bytecode.c and bytecode.h.
Updated the matching extern declaration in robj.c from extern float HSD_ByteCodeEval(u8*, float*, u32) to extern float HSD_ByteCodeEval(u8*, f32*, s32).

Reworked the local static inline fmodf helper: renamed parameters to divisor/dividend, introduced an explicit long long quotient local, used an early return when fabs(divisor) > fabs(dividend), and computed dividend - divisor * quotient.
Adjusted HSD_ByteCodeEval local declarations, including splitting HSD_SList* stack and list declarations, using int for loop index i, and using s32 for operand_count, d0, and d1.
Removed extra nested scopes around atan2f/RAD_TO_DEG handling while preserving the fabsf_bitwise(f0) == 0.0F special case that returns +/-90.0F.
Simplified boolean XOR-like integer logic for bytecode case 0x32 into a direct expression: (d0 == 0 && d1 != 0) || (d0 != 0 && d1 == 0).
In the fmod bytecode case, avoided storing the second stack float in f1 and passed ((ByteCodeVal*) &stack->data)->f directly to fmodf.
smart_moves: Normalized public and external signatures consistently across the implementation, header, and robj.c call-site declaration, avoiding mismatched prototype drift.
Used platform typedefs f32 and s32 where the decompiled SDK-style code likely expects them, improving both consistency and matching chances.
Introduced an explicit quotient local in fmodf rather than relying on a dense expression, which can influence register allocation and instruction scheduling for matching.
Removed artificial nested blocks and temporary booleans once they were no longer needed for matching, leaving clearer source while preserving equivalent codegen.
Kept the atan2f zero-divisor special case explicit, preserving behavior for f0 == 0.0F instead of reducing it to a naïve atan2f-only expression.
decomp_lessons: For matching work, function signatures must be synchronized in the .c implementation, exported header, and any local extern declarations; even integer signedness like u32 versus s32 can matter.
Small local declaration changes, including declaration order and splitting combined declarations, can be part of a successful match when they affect stack layout or register allocation.
Equivalent boolean logic can often be collapsed after matching pressure is resolved; direct C expressions may still match better than compiler-artifact-style temporary bool chains.
Inline helper functions are matching-sensitive: parameter order/names do not affect semantics, but expression shape, temporaries, casts, and early returns can affect generated assembly.
When manipulating stack data through ByteCodeVal casts and HSD_SListRemove, avoid introducing unnecessary temporaries if the target code appears to read directly from the current stack node.
naming_conventions: HSD_ByteCodeEval uses HSD_ prefix and CamelCase SDK-style naming.
Platform typedefs f32 and s32 were preferred over raw float and int in the matched signature.
The helper kept the standard-library-like name fmodf as a static inline local implementation, with parameters renamed to divisor and dividend in this PR.
Existing bytecode locals use short decomp-style names such as fv, f0, f1, d0, d1, operand_count, and last_command.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed fmodf from a single conditional-expression implementation into explicit control flow with a long long quotient local.
Changed HSD_ByteCodeEval local variable types and declaration ordering to improve match behavior.
Removed temporary f1 in one fmod path and passed the stack-derived float directly.
Flattened nested scopes around case 0x2F atan2f logic.
Replaced a multi-step bool/temporary implementation of an exclusive-or-style condition with a direct boolean expression.
Ran verification commands reported in the PR body: ninja -j8, clang-format pre-commit on bytecode.c/bytecode.h/robj.c, and pre-commit run on those files.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.comment: idk why codex insisted on renaming this everywhere. can clean up if preferred
review_feedback.interpretation: The only review-comment evidence is from the author noting that an automated/Codex-assisted rename may have been unnecessary and offering to clean it up. No reviewer-requested change is visible in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching HSD_ByteCodeEval to see whether the Codex-driven renaming mentioned by the author was kept or cleaned up.
Search for other baselib functions with local extern declarations that may need synchronization with headers.
Compare other inline math helper matches for patterns involving explicit quotient locals and f32/s32 typedef normalization.
